context.description: A single-page application designed to help users build structured, high-fidelity JSON prompts for LLMs by defining system specifications through a modular form interface.
context.agent_role: Senior Product Designer and React Architect

ensure: Real-time synchronization between form inputs and JSON preview
Responsive layout for desktop and mobile
Collapsible and toggleable sections for granular control
Clean, modern aesthetic with zinc and orange accents
separate: Types and interfaces in a dedicated file

state_changes: Form input change updates the central state object
Section toggle checkbox sets the corresponding top-level key to null or default values
Copy button triggers clipboard write and temporary success state
validation: Framework selection 'Other' enables custom text input
List inputs require non-empty strings to add items
edge_cases: Handling null sections in the UI to prevent rendering errors
Mobile view collapsing the grid to a single column

test_cases: Verify that disabling a section removes it from the JSON preview
Ensure custom framework input correctly populates the framework field
Check that 'Copy' functionality works across different browsers
error_handling: Graceful fallback for missing section data using optional chaining
Visual feedback for failed actions (if any)
performance: Memoized JSON stringification to prevent unnecessary re-renders
